data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on JFrog, Cites Strong Growth Outlook and Competitive Moat; Price Target Maintained at $78
data.description:
Analyst Shrenik Kothari of Robert W. Baird maintained a Buy rating on JFrog, retaining the price target of $78.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to JFrog’s growth outlook and risk‑reward profile. He sees Q1 and FY26 set up favorably, with achievable cloud growth targets, a history of outperformance even if beat magnitude moderates, and a model where committed cloud contracts limit downside while incremental usage, security attach, and large migrations provide upside. Valuation is viewed as reasonable relative to the company’s consistent execution and its medium‑term margin and free‑cash‑flow targets, supporting a constructive stance.

Kothari also emphasizes JFrog’s competitive moat in Artifactory and its role as an independent control plane for software binaries, which becomes more important as AI‑driven coding expands binary creation and movement. He highlights strong customer and partner feedback on the platform, early but growing traction in security (especially Curation) amid rising supply‑chain and open‑source attacks, and believes these dynamics should sustain robust cloud demand and reinforce the company’s long‑term strategic position. The price target is unchanged at $78 (prior $78).

In another report released yesterday, BTIG also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $60.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Flowserve: Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Sees Temporary Headwinds and Trims Price Target to $85 on Delayed Orders
data.description:
Analyst Joseph C Giordano from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Flowserve and decreased the price target to $85.00 from $100.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joseph C Giordano has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including his view that the current Middle East–related disruption is a temporary setback that ultimately should translate into a stronger, multi‑year demand environment for Flowserve. While he acknowledges near‑term execution and communication missteps that have put pressure on the shares, he notes that management is already seeing normalization in key North American maintenance and repair activity, supporting the case that recent softness is transitory.

Giordano also emphasizes that his longer‑term earnings projections through FY27 are only modestly adjusted, reflecting a slight delay in order timing rather than a deterioration in the fundamental outlook. He maintains that the company is well positioned to benefit from an improved order environment in 2027 and 2028, and his revised $85 price target, based on mid‑ to high‑teens forward P/E multiples, still offers enough upside to justify a Buy rating despite the nearer‑term uncertainty and investor caution.

In another report released today, Robert W. Baird also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $91.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Cboe Kept at Hold as Strong Earnings and Strategic Shift Are Offset by Market-Share Pressures and Premium Valuation
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jeff Schmitt has maintained their neutral stance on CBOE stock, giving a Hold rating on April 22.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jeff Schmitt has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, balancing Cboe’s strong recent performance with emerging headwinds. The company substantially exceeded earnings expectations and benefited from higher market volatility, while its strategic shift toward core franchises and new initiatives such as prediction markets should support profitability over time.

At the same time, Cboe’s multi-listed options and flagship index options segments are facing declining market share, difficult year-over-year comparisons, and rising competitive pressures, which may temper future derivatives growth unless volatility stays unusually high. With the share price already reflecting much of the upside from the business realignment and now trading at a premium multiple to its historical valuation, Schmitt sees limited near-term upside, leading him to maintain a neutral, Hold stance on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Schmitt is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 8.6% and a 54.13% success rate. Schmitt covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Tradeweb Markets, Marketaxess Holdings, and Nasdaq.

In another report released on April 22, Bank of America Securities also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $330.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Cloudflare: Buy Rating Reiterated, $260 Price Target Maintained on Strengthening AI, Zero Trust, and Workers Growth Drivers
data.description:
Cloudflare, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Shrenik Kothari from Robert W. Baird maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $260.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Cloudflare’s near-term setup and longer-term growth drivers. He highlights that the company is entering 1Q with its easiest revenue comparison of the year, supportive demand checks around AI, SASE, and Pool-of-Funds, and a clearer understanding among investors of normal Q1 seasonality following a strong Q4, all of which underpin expectations for solid execution and usage reacceleration through the rest of the year. The unchanged $260 price target reflects confidence that revenue growth can accelerate quarter over quarter in FY25, supported by ramping PoF consumption and Workers’ growing role as the compute layer for agentic and ephemeral workloads, even as valuation and year‑to‑date outperformance keep the bar for upside relatively high.

At the same time, Kothari sees potential upside to management’s conservative profitability guidance, particularly around sales and marketing leverage relative to long-term targets, which could enhance operating margins over time. Field checks reinforce the bullish view, indicating robust momentum in AI-related traffic, Zero Trust adoption, and Workers usage, while management’s conviction in rebuilding a cadence of large enterprise deals suggests that the multi-act growth flywheel continues to resonate with customers and supports sustained, premium‑like growth characteristics despite near-term investor debates on valuation.

In another report released on April 26, Citi also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $265.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Asure Software: Buy Rating Reiterated on Strong 2026 Start and Platform Momentum; $11 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
In a report released today, Bryan Bergin from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Asure, with a price target of $11.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bryan Bergin has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Asure’s strong start to 2026 with revenue and adjusted EBITDA exceeding expectations and a slight increase in full-year growth guidance while maintaining margin targets. He also highlights accelerating organic growth, a sizable contracted backlog with near-term conversion potential, and stable client behavior with no clear signs of macro-driven weakness.

In addition, Bergin points to the strategic benefits of migrating most customers to the Asure Central platform, which should support higher cross-sell activity, rising product attach rates, and meaningful PEPM expansion over the next few years, aided by newer offerings like AsureWorks. Despite recent share outperformance in a generally out-of-favor HCM sector and ongoing AI-related sentiment swings, he views the current valuation of under 2x CY27E EV/S as presenting an attractive risk-reward, and he keeps his price target unchanged at $11.

In another report released today, Lake Street also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $13.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: TriMas Maintained at Buy as Analyst Reaffirms $45 Price Target on Strong Balance Sheet and Earnings Power
data.description:
BWS Financial analyst Hamed Khorsand has maintained their bullish stance on TRS stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hamed Khorsand has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including TriMas’s strong balance sheet and earnings power. The company exited the first quarter of 2026 with a substantial net cash position and has two business units that are expected to each contribute meaningfully to adjusted EBITDA by 2027, underscoring a solid long‑term earnings profile.

TriMas also exceeded his revenue expectations in the latest quarter, with part of the upside tied to tooling sales that should translate into future project revenue, reinforcing forward visibility. In addition, shares currently trade at an enterprise value that appears low relative to the company’s projected adjusted EBITDA and anticipated cost-driven margin improvements, while ongoing share repurchases and potential strategic interest from acquirers further support his unchanged $45 price target and Buy view.
